# Assisted Stretches

> Brisbane's dedicated one-on-one assisted stretching studio. 60-minute sessions using PNF (proprioceptive neuromuscular facilitation) and passive stretching techniques to improve flexibility, accelerate recovery, and regulate the nervous system. 4.9/5 across 180+ Google reviews. 2,400+ sessions delivered. Health fund rebates available via HICAPS on-site.

## Services

- [Single Session](/): One-on-one 60-minute assisted stretch session — $125. Full-body PNF and passive stretching targeting hamstrings, hips, shoulders, and lower back. First-session money-back guarantee.
- [5-Session Pack](/): Five 60-minute sessions — $575 ($115/session). Save $50 vs single sessions. Valid 6 months.
- [10-Session Pack](/): Ten 60-minute sessions — $1,000 ($100/session). Save $250 vs single sessions. Valid 12 months.
- [Gift Cards](/): Digital gift cards for 1, 5, or 10 sessions. From $125. Delivered instantly. Valid 12 months.
- [FAQ](/): Common questions about assisted stretching — technique, frequency, clothing, health fund rebates.

## Key Facts

- Business type: Health and wellness studio — assisted stretching
- Location: Brisbane, Queensland, Australia
- Session duration: 60 minutes
- Technique: PNF (proprioceptive neuromuscular facilitation), passive and active assisted stretching
- Sessions delivered: 2,400+

## What Is Assisted Stretching?

Assisted stretching is a one-on-one therapy where a trained practitioner moves the client's body through a sequence of held and dynamic stretches. Unlike solo stretching, the client's muscles can fully release because they are not controlling the movement. PNF (proprioceptive neuromuscular facilitation) uses contract-relax cycles to access ranges of motion the body normally protects, producing deeper and longer-lasting results than passive stretching alone.

## Who It Is For

- Office workers with chronic tightness from prolonged sitting
- Athletes seeking faster recovery between training sessions
- People with lower back, hip, or shoulder stiffness
- Anyone who wants to move better and feel less restricted

## Frequently Asked Questions

Q: Will assisted stretching hurt?
A: No. Pressure is always within your comfort. We work to a "good stretch" feeling — if it ever tips into pain, we back off.

Q: Do I need to be flexible to benefit?
A: No. The less flexible you are, the more dramatic the gain. Tightness is the reason to come, not the reason to wait.

Q: What do I wear?
A: Gym shorts and a t-shirt, or anything you can move freely in. No need to undress.

Q: How often should I come?
A: Most clients see best results once a week or fortnight. Athletes in heavy training blocks come twice a week.

Q: Is it covered by health funds?
A: Many extras policies cover remedial elements of the session through HICAPS on-site. Check with your fund before booking.

Q: What is PNF stretching?
A: Proprioceptive neuromuscular facilitation uses contract-relax cycles to access ranges your body normally protects. The result is deeper, faster, longer-lasting change than passive stretching alone.
